title: A Career in Document Search
article_text: I saw a huge document on my desk this morning, and I needed to check every pattern in it. The application for my new job was unclear, so I asked my officer for help. Obviously, I had to search for the central information about my career. My boss gave me a bill for the new office dress, and I was hot from the work. I heard a dog bark throughout the speech, but I continued anyway. I had to separate the oil documents from the profit reports. I made a guess about the correct answer, and it was correct. The style of the report was interesting, but I needed to refer to the example. For instance, I saw a message about the new application in the central office. I went to the dress shop quickly because I needed a new shirt for the career meeting.

sentences.zh: 今天早上我看到一份巨大的文件在我的桌子上，我需要检查其中的每一个模式。

vocabulary.eng_def: a paper or electronic record with information

vocabulary.eng_def: to divide something into parts or not together

vocabulary.eng_def: a formal talk given to an audience

vocabulary.eng_def: a common animal with four legs, kept as a pet or for work
vocabulary.example: My dog likes to play in the park.

vocabulary.eng_def: a person with a position of authority in an organization
vocabulary.example: The police officer helped us.

vocabulary.eng_def: in every part of a place or during a whole period of time
vocabulary.example: It rained throughout the day.

vocabulary.eng_def: a thick liquid from under the ground used for fuel or cooking
vocabulary.example: We need oil to cook the food.

vocabulary.eng_def: a piece of clothing for women or the act of putting on clothes
vocabulary.example: She wore a red dress to the party.

vocabulary.eng_def: money that you get from selling something for more than it cost
vocabulary.example: The company made a big profit.

vocabulary.eng_def: to give an answer without knowing if it is true
vocabulary.example: Can you guess my age?
